description.en: Telling the time — clock time and the time prepositions om, op, and in.

rules.shortcut: The BIG trap: 'half drie' = 2:30, NOT 3:30. Dutch 'half' means halfway TO the next hour.
rules.explanation: Telling time in Dutch looks simple but has one major trap: 'half' works differently. Dutch counts forward TO the next hour, not backward from the current one. Master that and the rest follows logically.

examples: Het is twee uur. (It is two o'clock.)
Het is twaalf uur. (It is twelve o'clock.)
Het is middernacht. (It is midnight.)
's ochtends / 's middags / 's avonds = in the morning / afternoon / evening
Het is drie uur 's middags. (It is 3pm.)
Het is kwart over twee. (It is quarter past two.) = 2:15
Het is kwart over tien. (It is quarter past ten.) = 10:15

patterns.rule: Half — WARNING: counts TO the next hour!
examples: Het is half drie. = 2:30 ← halfway TO three, NOT half past two!
Het is half twaalf. = 11:30
Het is half één. = 12:30
🚫 NEVER say 'half twee' for 2:30 — that is 1:30!
Het is kwart voor drie. (It is quarter to three.) = 2:45
Het is kwart voor twaalf. (It is quarter to twelve.) = 11:45
Het is vijf over twee. = 2:05
Het is tien over twee. = 2:10
Het is tien voor half drie. = 2:20 (ten to half three)
Het is vijf voor half drie. = 2:25 (five to half three)
Het is vijf over half drie. = 2:35 (five past half three)
Het is tien voor drie. = 2:50
Het is vijf voor drie. = 2:55

patterns.rule: Om hoe laat? — At what time?
examples: Om hoe laat begint de les? (At what time does the lesson start?)
De les begint om negen uur. (The lesson starts at nine o'clock.)
Om hoe laat eindigt de les? (At what time does the lesson end?)
Om hoe laat vertrekt de trein? (At what time does the train leave?)

rules.memory: 🧠 'half [X]' = halfway TO [X]. So half drie = halfway to three = 2:30. Think of it as a progress bar: ▓▓▓▓▓░░░░░ toward the next hour.

examples.nl: Om hoe laat begint de les?
examples.en: At what time does the lesson start?

examples.nl: De les begint om half negen.

practice.nl: Het is half drie. (NOT half twee!)
Om hoe laat begint de les?
practice.en: At what time does the lesson start?

practice.nl: Om hoe laat eindigt de les?
practice.en: At what time does the lesson end?

rules.shortcut: Om = clock time. Op = specific day or date. In = period, month, season, year.
rules.explanation: Dutch uses three different prepositions for time, each with a specific job. Getting them mixed up is one of the most common errors at this level. The good news: each one has a clear rule that covers almost all cases.

examples: Om 7 uur. (At 7 o'clock.)
Om half 8. (At 7:30.) ← 'op half 8' is also heard but om is standard
Om kwart over tien. (At quarter past ten.)
Om middernacht. (At midnight.)
De les begint om 9 uur. (The lesson starts at 9 o'clock.)
Ik sta op om 7 uur 's morgens. (I get up at 7 o'clock in the morning.)
↳ Rule: if you can put a clock face next to it → use OM

patterns.rule: OP — specific days, dates, and named moments
examples: Op maandag. (On Monday.)
Op dinsdag. (On Tuesday.)
Op maandagavond. (On Monday evening.)
Op 8 september. (On 8 September.)
Op 8 september 2021. (On 8 September 2021.)
Op mijn verjaardag. (On my birthday.)
Op Kerstmis. (On Christmas.)
↳ Rule: specific calendar point — a named day, date, or occasion → use OP
In het weekend. (At/in the weekend.)
In de vakantie. (During the holidays.)
In de zomer. (In summer.)
In de herfst. (In autumn.)
In maart. (In March.)
In de ochtend. (In the morning.) ← note: 's ochtends is also common
↳ Rule: stretched period of time, not a single point → use IN

patterns.rule: Side-by-side comparison — the same idea, different prepositions
examples: OP maandag (on Monday — specific day) vs IN de week (in the week — period)
OP 8 september (on 8th September — specific date) vs IN september (in September — the whole month)
OM 7 uur (at 7 o'clock — precise time) vs IN de ochtend (in the morning — period)
OP maandagochtend (on Monday morning — named specific time) vs IN de ochtend (in general)
om ___ uur 's morgens / 's ochtends (at ___ o'clock in the morning)
op ___ avond (on ___ evening) — op vrijdagavond (on Friday evening)
in het weekend (at the weekend)
in de vakantie (during the holidays)
in de zomer / winter / lente / herfst (in summer / winter / spring / autumn)
in [month] — in januari, in februari, in maart...
in [year] — in 2021, in 1999

rules.memory: 🧠 Om = O'clock (both start with O — easy!). Op = a point you can point to on a calendar (specific day/date). In = inside a period of time (month, season, year, weekend).

examples.nl: In het weekend. / In de zomer. / In maart. / In 1999.
examples.en: At the weekend. / In summer. / In March. / In 1999.

examples.nl: De les is op dinsdag om half tien.
examples.en: The lesson is on Tuesday at 9:30.

practice.nl: Om, op of in? → '___ het weekend'
practice.en: Om, op or in? → '___ the weekend'

practice.nl: IN het weekend — een periode, geen specifiek punt.
practice.en: IN het weekend — a period, not a specific point.

practice.nl: Om, op of in? → '___ de zomer'
IN de zomer — een seizoen is een periode.
practice.en: IN de zomer — a season is a period.

practice.nl: De les is op ___ om ___ uur.
practice.en: The lesson is on ___ at ___ o'clock.
When do you go on holiday?
